Greenpeace's Dai Yun says e-waste is a global problem due to the fast-growing electronic industry and people tossing out old electronics. 4. 20-50 million tons of e-waste are produced globally each year. 5. Components in electronics have harmful chemicals that pollute groundwater and the environment. 6. The U.S. has no federal law for e-waste disposal, though some states have recycling programs. 7. The U.S. exports e-waste to third-world countries like India and China. 8. Workers there take apart computers for valuable parts, but this practice is dangerous (burning or using acid to extract metals). 9. Dr. Bakul Rao says this method is dangerous because workers are not educated and don't use proper health systems, leading to exposure to harmful substances.
